about:
Siye Wu (伍思烨) is a second-year M.S. student in Computer Science at Fudan University, advised by [Prof. Yanghua Xiao](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=odFW4FoAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao).

His research interests lie in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Large Language Models (LLMs), with a focus on:

- **Efficient LLMs** — Designing computation- and budget-aware LLMs that emphasize adaptive inference, reasoning efficiency, and performance–cost trade-offs across tasks of varying complexity.
- **LLM Post-training and Reasoning** — Developing post-training methods, such as supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL), to improve LLMs' reasoning capabilities.

publications.name: CODA: Difficulty-Aware Compute Allocation for Adaptive Reasoning
publications.authors: Siye Wu, Jian Xie, Yikai Zhang, Yanghua Xiao.

publications.name: Step 3.5 Flash: Open Frontier-Level Intelligence with 11B Active Parameters

publications.name: ARM: Adaptive Reasoning Model
publications.authors: Siye Wu, Jian Xie, Yikai Zhang, Aili Chen, Kai Zhang, Yu Su, Yanghua Xiao.

publications.name: How Easily Do Irrelevant Inputs Skew the Responses of Large Language Models?
publications.authors: Siye Wu, Jian Xie, Jiangjie Chen, Tinghui Zhu, Kai Zhang, Yanghua Xiao.
